description_en: Chinese Ming-style blue and white porcelain vase (double-eared form) with a smooth, glossy glazed surface and underglaze cobalt blue decoration

components.name: vase_body
components.description: main bulbous porcelain body with tapered foot and flared rim, formed by revolving a curved profile

components.name: foot_ring
components.description: un-glazed ceramic foot ring at the base, slightly wider than the body bottom

components.name: rim_ring
components.description: thickened lip ring at the opening

components.name: left_handle
components.description: ear-shaped loop handle connecting shoulder to neck
components.position_note: left side of body at shoulder height

components.name: right_handle
components.description: ear-shaped loop handle connecting shoulder to neck
components.position_note: right side of body at shoulder height

style_notes: Classic blue-and-white (qinghua) porcelain: white kaolin body with cobalt oxide painted decoration depicting scrolling lotus or landscape scenes, covered by transparent high-gloss glaze. The foot ring remains un-glazed to prevent sticking during firing. Handles follow the 'double-ear' (shuang'er) form typical of Ming dynasty altar vases. Proportions follow the 3:2 height-to-belly-width ratio.
